docs.title: Lesson overview
docs.text:
Description This course is providing introduction to the TITLE OF THE COURSE, further description of the courses.

Prerequisites To be able to follow this course, learners should have knowledge of:  1. First requirement  2. Second requirement

Learning Outcomes: By the end of the course, learners will be able to:  1. First item  2. Second item

Target Audience: The training materials are meant for:

  1. For self paced learning
  2. For trainers to use it to prepare their training
  3. For community to collect the knowledge about this topic in one place.

Level: Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced

Time plan: The content will approximately follow this time plan: 00-15 min 15-30 min 30-45 min 45-60 min 60-75 min 75-90 min * 105-120 min

ARISE competencies covered by this course:

 1. First competency  2. Second competency  3. Third competency

License: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License

Funding: ARISE2 project has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon Europe’s research and innovation programme under the Marie Skłodowska-Curie COFUND, grant agreement No. 101178241.

docs.title: Citing this lesson
docs.text:
Please cite as:

  1. Authors. (year). Title of the course. Zenodo. DOI.
  2. e.g. Geert van Geest, Elin Kronander, Jose Alejandro Romero Herrera, Nadja Žlender, & Alexia Cardona. (2023). The ELIXIR Training Lesson Template - Developing Training Together (v1.0.0-alpha). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7913092.

docs.text:
Here’s a lit of used keywords:

  1. URL: https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btt113, arXiv:https://academic.oup.com/bioinformatics/article-pdf/29/10/1325/710075/btt113.pdf, doi:10.1093/bioinformatics/btt113.mkdocs ↩

docs.title: 1.1 First subtopic
docs.text:
Here you can enter text and create inline citations1 by using the bibtex plugin. Add your references in references.bib, and cite 2 by adding the @refid inside brackets like this ``

test

You can also embed videos from a local source (with a relative path) or from an url (like youtube). To use a youtube URL, just attach the ID of the video to a youtube embedded video link: https://youtube.com/embed/. For example, the Elixir training video https://youtu.be/oAD8FdGf8tI has the ID oAD8FdGf8tI, so the final link would be:

![type:video](https://youtube.com/embed/oAD8FdGf8tI)

docs.title: This is how to add an image from an online resource
docs.text:
If you want to add an image, the best way would be to use html, e.g:

<figure>
<img src="https://elixir-europe.org/sites/default/files/ebif_news_release_image_news_landingpageimage_.png" width="600" alt="Image on elixir landing page"/>
<figcaption> Elixir image </figcaption>
</figure>

docs.title: This is how to add an image which needs to be uploaded to the repository (not available online)
docs.text:
If you want to use local images, add them to docs/assets/images and refer to them in the html as a relative path, e.g.:

<figure>
<img src="../../assets/images/elixir_image.png" width="600" alt="Image on elixir landing page"/>
<figcaption> Elixir image </figcaption>
</figure>

docs.title: This is how to add references
docs.text: In order to add a reference, add it in bibtex format to references.bib located in the main directory, cite it in your markdown document with `` and cite 1 like this:
